title: Best CRM for Small Business
description: Compare HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive, and Freshsales to find the best CRM for your small business needs and budget.
body:
Small businesses need a CRM that grows with them without requiring a dedicated admin or a six-figure annual commitment. Here is how the top four platforms compare across pricing, ease of use, and feature sets.

## HubSpot CRM

HubSpot's free tier remains the best entry point for small businesses. It includes contact management, deal tracking, email tracking (up to 2,000 emails/month), and a meeting scheduler — enough for a solo founder or a team of five to run a structured sales process. The paid plans start at $50/month per seat for Sequences, custom reporting, and workflow automation. HubSpot's ecosystem includes a free CMS, marketing hub, and service desk, making it the obvious choice if you plan to consolidate your go-to-market tools under one roof. The trade-off: once you outgrow the free tier, costs can escalate quickly as you add users and premium add-ons.

## Salesforce

Salesforce is the most powerful CRM on the market, but it is overkill for most small businesses. The Starter Suite ($25/user/month) covers lead management, account hierarchies, and pipeline forecasting, but configuring it properly usually requires a consultant. Where Salesforce shines for small businesses is its AppExchange marketplace — if your niche industry has a vertical-specific app (e.g., real estate, healthcare, nonprofits), Salesforce likely has a tailored solution. Only consider Salesforce if you have a dedicated sales operations person or a specific compliance requirement like HIPAA that other CRMs handle poorly.

| Feature | HubSpot Free | Pipedrive | Freshsales | Salesforce Starter |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price (per user/month) | $0 | $14 | $9 | $25 |
| Deal pipeline management | Yes | Yes (customizable stages) | Yes | Yes |
| Email integration | Yes (tracking) | Yes (sync + tracking) | Yes (built-in) | Yes (via add-on) |
| Workflow automation | Limited (paid) | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Native phone | No | No | Yes | No |

## Pipedrive

Pipedrive is built around the pipeline-first philosophy — every screen is designed to move deals forward. It is ideal for small sales teams that follow a predictable B2B sales process with defined stages. The visual deal board, activity reminders, and email sync are straightforward. In 2026, Pipedrive introduced AI-powered deal scoring that highlights which opportunities are most likely to close based on historical activity patterns. The main limitation: Pipedrive's reporting and marketing features lag behind HubSpot's, so you will likely need separate tools for email campaigns and analytics.

## Freshsales

Freshsales (part of the Freshworks suite) offers the best value for bootstrapped teams. At $9/user/month, it includes a built-in phone system, email, lead scoring, and appointment scheduling — features that cost extra on other platforms. The AI assistant, Freddy, surfaces the next best action for each lead and auto-enriches contacts with company data. Freshsales works well for B2C or high-volume sales teams that need to call and email leads directly from the CRM without switching tabs.

## Final Recommendation

Start with HubSpot's free tier if you are brand new to CRM and want the most generous free offering. Move to Pipedrive or Freshsales once you outgrow HubSpot's limitations and need pipeline automation or built-in calling. Skip Salesforce until you have dedicated ops staff or a specific regulatory need. The best CRM for a small business is the one your team actually uses daily — prioritize adoption over feature checklists.
author: StackPilot Team
